# Runoff 08 — CA Governor, a real STAR field of 61 (BV2181, gvdy42)

*Generated from [`Runoff_08_ca_governor_reversal_gvdy42.yaml`](../Runoff_08_ca_governor_reversal_gvdy42.yaml) — do not edit by hand. Regenerate: `python STARVote_LH_tabulation_engine/tools_adam/scripts/build_yaml_pages.py`.*

**Method:** [STAR (single winner)](../../../00_start_here/STAR_Voting) · **1 seat** · **Expected winner:** Tom Steyer (D)

## Scenario

A REAL BetterVoting STAR demo — the "2026 California Governor Election", 61
candidates, 319 ballots (302 tallied, 17 abstentions). Three lessons in one live
election: (1) RUNOFF REVERSAL — Alicia Olivia Lapp (R) tops the score round
(395 vs Tom Steyer's 390), but Steyer wins the automatic runoff 98-80, so STAR
elects the majority-preferred finalist, not the highest scorer. (2) LH<->BV
EQUAL SUPPORT reconciliation — LH reports 141 Equal Support in the runoff, BV
124; the gap is exactly the 17 abstentions (141 = 124 + 17), which LH counts as
Equal Support and BV sets aside (see reporting_diff_BV_LH.md). (3) The #1390
reporting bug — BV's "Distribution of Equal Support" graph showed only 5 of the
124, dropping ballots that skipped BOTH finalists (null==null) and null-vs-0
ballots; fixed by coercing skipped scores to 0 in PR #1431. Winner: Tom Steyer (D).
Live results: https://bettervoting.com/gvdy42/results
Lesson: Runoff_08_ca_governor_reversal_gvdy42.md

## Ballots

Row 1 = candidate names; each later row is one voter's 0–5 scores (a `N ×` prefix = N identical ballots).

Markers on these ballots: `-` blank · `~` race abstention · `&` candidate abstention · `?` spoiled · `%` spoiled+reissued — all tabulate as 0 (reported honestly).
